class.name: PuertsScriptValue
class.brief_description: Wraps a live value inside a [PuertsEnvironment].

method.name: is_valid
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the script value still points to a live value in a live [PuertsEnvironment].

method.name: is_null
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is [code]null[/code]. If the script value is no longer valid, this method also returns [code]true[/code].

method.name: is_undefined
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is [code]undefined[/code]. If the script value is no longer valid, this method also returns [code]true[/code].

method.name: is_bool
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is a script boolean.

method.name: is_int
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is an integer type.

method.name: is_float
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is a floating-point number.

method.name: is_string
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is a string.

method.name: is_binary
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is binary data.

method.name: is_object
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is a script object.

method.name: is_function
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the wrapped script value is callable as a function.

method.name: to_bool
method.description: Returns the wrapped value as a Godot [bool] using direct typed access.

method.name: to_int
method.description: Returns the wrapped script value as a Godot [int] using direct typed access.

method.name: to_float
method.description: Returns the wrapped script value as a Godot [float] using direct typed access.

method.name: to_string
method.description: Returns the wrapped script value as a Godot [String] using direct typed access.

method.name: to_binary
method.description: Returns the wrapped script value as a Godot [PackedByteArray] using direct typed access.

method.name: to_native
method.description: Converts the script value to the closest Godot [Variant] representation. Primitive values, strings, binary values, and script native Godot values are converted directly. Binary values become [PackedByteArray]. If the value cannot be represented directly, Puerts keeps it wrapped so you can continue to access it through [PuertsScriptValue]. If conversion fails, this method returns [code]null[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Prefer a more specific conversion method when you already know the expected type. For example, use [method to_int], [method to_float], [method to_string], [method to_binary], or [method unwrap_native] when those methods match your data. [method to_native] performs additional type checks so it can handle many value kinds, which makes it more flexible but also more expensive than a direct conversion. [codeblocks] [gdscript] var vec_value := env.eval("new Vector2(3, 4)") var vec: Vector2 = vec_value.to_native() print(vec.length()) # 5 [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

method.name: unwrap_native
method.description: Returns the original Godot value wrapped inside the script value. Use this when the script side is holding a native Godot object or a static-bound built-in value such as [code]Vector2[/code]. This includes values passed into Puerts earlier and built-in values created through [code]load_type()[/code]. If unwrapping fails, this method returns [code]null[/code].

method.name: get_property
method.description: Returns the property named [param name] from the wrapped object. The property value stays wrapped as a new [PuertsScriptValue].

method.name: set_property
method.description: Sets the property named [param name] on the wrapped object to [param value]. The [Variant] is converted to a script value before assignment.

method.name: call
method.description: Calls the wrapped value as a function with [param args]. Arguments are converted from Godot [Variant] values to script values. If the wrapped value is not callable, or if the call throws an exception, this method returns [code]null[/code] and records the error on the parent environment.

method.name: call_method
method.description: Calls the method named [param name] on the wrapped object with [param args]. The wrapped object is used as the receiver, so script-side [code]this[/code] semantics stay intact. If the property is missing or is not callable, this method returns [code]null[/code] and records an error on the parent environment.
